rationale: FZ-036a Rename README badges and repo URLs — no changes required.

What is done:
- README.md badges and URLs already point to easel/fizeau:
  - Line 5: CI badge → github.com/easel/fizeau/actions/workflows/ci.yml
  - Line 6: Release badge → github.com/easel/fizeau/releases/latest
  - Line 19: install.sh raw URL → raw.githubusercontent.com/easel/fizeau/master/install.sh
  - Line 25: go install path → github.com/easel/fizeau/cmd/fiz
  - Lines 93–94: import paths → github.com/easel/fizeau, .../configinit
  - Lines 181, 183: docs links → easel.github.io/fizeau
- DDx link on line 12 points to DocumentDrivenDX/ddx (correct sibling repo).
- `git remote -v` confirms origin is easel/fizeau.
- No stale ddx-agent or non-DocumentDrivenDX fizeau URLs remain in README.md
  (verified via grep for `ddx-agent` and `github.com/<owner>/fizeau`).

Acceptance criterion: "README badges/URLs point to easel/fizeau or
are explicitly deferred by FZ-004." — satisfied as-is.

Why no commit:
- FZ-036a was likely covered transitively by sibling rename beads (FZ-036b
  website, FZ-036c install URLs, FZ-035a README modernization) before this
  bead was claimed. The README is already in the target state; making a
  no-op commit would be churn. No follow-up needed.

What a follow-up attempt would need:
- Nothing for the stated AC. If a separate concern is added (e.g., the
  Release badge SVG URL on line 6 is not a real shields.io endpoint and
  will 404 — switching to https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/easel/fizeau
  would render properly), file it as a new bead; that goes beyond a rename.
